title: Continuous control monitoring anomaly — failure-rate trending up
exclusions: Does NOT replace control deficiency root-cause analysis.
evidence: Saved search 'ccm_failure_trend' (daily) emits controls whose 7-day failure rate exceeds +3σ of the 90-day baseline.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CA-7 continuous monitoring; anomaly detection is the operational expression.
A.5.36 compliance with policies, rules, and standards.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Control MFA-PCI-01 has a failure rate of 5% over the last 7 days vs baseline 0.5±0.2%; the UC fires.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=ccm:execution earliest=-90d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count(eval(result="fail")) as fails count as total by _time control_id
| eval fail_rate=fails/total
| eventstats avg(fail_rate) as avg7d by control_id
| stats avg(fail_rate) as baseline stdev(fail_rate) as baseline_sd latest(fail_rate) as current latest(avg7d) as avg7d by control_id
| where avg7d > baseline+3*baseline_sd
| table control_id baseline baseline_sd avg7d current
description: Surfaces controls whose failure rate is trending up — the most reliable early-warning signal that a control is drifting.
value: Gives internal audit a quantitative view of control health rather than a point-in-time pass/fail.
implementation: (1) Instrument CCM per control; (2) run daily; (3) open a deep-dive ticket for anomalies.
visualization: Line charts of failure rate with baseline bands, table of controls above threshold, single-value 'controls within baseline'.
knownFalsePositives: Controls with naturally bimodal distributions (business-hours only) benefit from a bucketed baseline.
